nodes.name: 得克萨斯州

nodes.name: 玫瑰琵鷺

links.evidence: For The First Time (It's Not A Flamingo) |url=https://dcist.com/story/21/08/04/tropical-pink-bird-spotted-in-d-c-for-the-first-time-its-not-a-flamingo/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804181848/https://dcist.com/story/21/08/04/tropical-pink-bird-spotted-in-d-c-for-the-first-time-its-not-a-flamingo/ |archive-date=4 August 2021 |access-date=4 August 2021 |website=DCist |language=en}} A large flock was spotted in Huntley Meadows Park in Fairfax County, Virginia, drawing a large crowd of spectators. The bird has also been spotted in New Jersey with increasing frequency. In Florida Bay, roseate spoonbills are an ecological and scientific indicator species
